title: Velvety Crispy Polish Cheese Gratin
description: A lazy-sunday Polish dish that is floral, crispy, and steam-rich, served straight from a mason jar.

ingredients.name: Grated kashkaval cheese
ingredients.notes: aged 2 weeks, cut into small cubes

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and lightly grease a mason jar with olive oil.
Whisk milk, baking powder, salt, pepper, and 2 tbsp honey together until smooth.
Layer the bottom of the jar with a thin coat of grated kashkaval cheese, sprinkle a bit of breadcrumbs if desired, and repeat until the jar is nearly full.
Pour the milk mixture over the cheese layers, ensuring it reaches the top of the jar.
Sprinkle diced smoked bacon and crushed walnuts over the top of the mixture.
Drizzle remaining honey over the surface to create a floral glaze.
Close the jar with its lid, place it on a baking sheet, and bake for 20 minutes until the top turns golden.
Remove the jar from the oven, let it rest for 5 minutes, and then gently open the lid.
Sprinkle fresh thyme leaves on top just before serving for an aromatic finish.
Slice a portion into a bowl, let the steam rise, and enjoy the velvety crispy gratin.
